review: I cannot believe my experience at R2L. I walked in with a ton dead bugs outside. I can understand this is out of the restaurant and buildings control. But when I walked through the rotating doors a large insect flew straight into my eye. I let it go because I understand this is probably still most likely out of the restaurants control. We were greeted nicely and directed where to go upstairs. When we got there at 8 for my reservation time, the hostess said a table was not yet ready and we could take a seat at the bar. It was not a big deal at first, but we were there for almost 15 minutes, when a fire alarm started blaring. The bartenders continued on with their jobs, which I found somewhat professional, but when I asked what the problem was, they nonchalantly said "I don't know" as fire alarms blasted in our ears. I asked the hostess if we could leave and I got an aggressive answer of "The elevator is not in service but we could take the stairs but then fire alarm will me done before we got down, and it should be done in 10 or less minutes". I would have walked down if my fiancée was not in heels. We waited for another 45 minutes at the bar with lights flashing with no easy access to get out. We sat at the bar and got drinks as alarms kept ringing and felt trapped. Over an hour later of our reservation time, we were seated with no mention of the events that just took place. The appetizers were good but my steak I ordered medium rare was grey throughout most of it. My fiancée got the "restaurant week" deal that included a sorbet with unruled fruit. I really can't believe that this nice of an establishment would be so passive to alarms blaring for almost an hour without working elevators to their customers. My ears are still ringing.
